county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1795 from Northumberland County. Named for Lycoming Creek. Williamsport was the 'Lumber Capital of the World' in the 1800s. West Branch Susquehanna River runs through county. First Little League Baseball World Series held here (1947). Timber, manufacturing, and agriculture.
county_metadata.research_tips: Lycoming County Historical Society and Thomas T. Taber Museum in Williamsport have outstanding genealogical collections and lumber era documentation. Recorder of Deeds has records from 1795. For pre-1795 check Northumberland County. The lumber industry made Williamsport one of America's wealthiest cities per capita in the 1880s; lumber baron family records are at the historical society. James V. Brown Library has genealogical materials. Lycoming College archives hold local manuscripts. German and Scotch-Irish settlers documented in Lutheran, Reformed, and Presbyterian records. The West Branch canal and railroad records document transportation workers. Millionaires Row in Williamsport documents the timber wealth era.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from 1795. Online index.
Wills, administrations from 1795.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: German Lutheran and Reformed, Scotch-Irish Presbyterian records. Timber community churches.
Williamsport Sun-Gazette. Lumber era news, vital records, and community coverage.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1804 from Lycoming County. Northern Tier on New York border. Timber, oil, and tanning industries. Bradford is the largest city (oil boom town). Kane is another significant town. Allegheny National Forest partially in county. Named for Thomas McKean, PA governor.
county_metadata.research_tips: McKean County Historical Society in Smethport has genealogical files and oil/lumber era records. Recorder of Deeds has records from 1826 (county organized). For pre-1804 check Lycoming County. Bradford became an oil boom town in the 1870s-1880s; oil industry records document workers. Tanning industry and timber records are at the historical society. Cross-reference with Potter and Elk counties. The NY border means checking Cattaraugus and Allegany County NY records. Lutheran, Presbyterian, and Methodist church records are primary sources. The Kinzua Dam displaced Seneca Nation communities.
county_metadata.heritage_book: History of the Counties of McKean, Elk, Cameron and Potter (1890)

Bradford and Smethport newspapers with oil boom era coverage.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1800 from Allegheny County. Northwestern PA. Named for General Hugh Mercer. Sharon and Farrell are industrial cities on the Shenango River. Steel and manufacturing industries. Agricultural areas in eastern portion. Thiel College in Greenville.
county_metadata.research_tips: Mercer County Historical Society in Mercer maintains genealogical files and industrial heritage records. Recorder of Deeds has records from 1800. For pre-1800 check Allegheny County. The Sharon-Farrell area had steel and manufacturing with immigrant workers from Eastern Europe and Italy. Agricultural eastern townships have different research strategies. Presbyterian and Methodist church records serve earlier families. Catholic parishes document later immigrants. Thiel College archives have local history. The Shenango Valley connects to Lawrence County research. Buhl Farm Park records document the Buhl family. Cross-reference with Crawford and Venango counties.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from county formation.
Wills, administrations from 1800.

Sharon Herald and Mercer County papers.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1789 from Cumberland and Northumberland counties. Juniata Valley location. Named for Thomas Mifflin, first PA governor. Iron industry and agriculture. Lewistown was a Pennsylvania Canal town. Small county with strong German and Scotch-Irish heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: Mifflin County Historical Society in Lewistown maintains genealogical files and iron industry records. Recorder of Deeds has records from 1789. For pre-1789 check Cumberland and Northumberland counties. The Juniata Valley settlement patterns connect to Huntingdon and Juniata counties. Iron furnace records document workers. German Reformed, Lutheran, and Presbyterian church records are primary sources. The Pennsylvania Canal era brought workers and merchants through. Amish and Mennonite communities in Kishacoquillas Valley (Big Valley) have separate record traditions. Cross-reference with Centre and Juniata counties.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from 1789.
Wills, administrations from 1789.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1836 from Northampton and Pike counties. Pocono Mountains region. Resort and tourism area from 1800s. Delaware Water Gap is a natural landmark. East Stroudsburg University (1893). Growing residential community with NYC commuters.
county_metadata.research_tips: Monroe County Historical Association in Stroudsburg maintains genealogical files and Pocono region records. Recorder of Deeds has records from 1836. For pre-1836 check Northampton and Pike counties. The resort industry generated unique records documenting hospitality workers. German and Scotch-Irish settlers in the valleys; Dutch and English along the Delaware River. Lutheran, Reformed, Presbyterian, and Methodist church records are primary. East Stroudsburg University archives have local history. The Pocono tourist industry from 1800s created employment records. Delaware Water Gap NRA has displaced community records. Cross-reference with Northampton and Pike counties.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1784 from Philadelphia County. Named for General Richard Montgomery. Suburban Philadelphia county. Welsh Tract settlement extended here. Valley Forge (1777-1778 Continental Army winter camp). Significant Quaker heritage. One of most populous PA counties.
county_metadata.research_tips: Montgomery County research benefits from the Montgomery County Archives in Norristown which holds deed, probate, and court records in a professional archival setting. The Historical Society of Montgomery County maintains genealogical collections. Recorder of Deeds has records from 1784 with LANDEX online access. For pre-1784 check Philadelphia County. Valley Forge National Historical Park has Revolutionary War research materials. Welsh Tract families (Lower Merion, Merion, Radnor) documented in Welsh-American heritage sources. Quaker meeting records at Friends Historical Library Swarthmore cover Montgomery County meetings. The county's suburban growth means multiple waves of settlement. Ursinus College archives have local manuscripts. Peter Wentz Farmstead records document colonial German family.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1784; LANDEX online system. Welsh Tract colonial records

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1850 from Columbia County. Smallest PA county by area (except Philadelphia). Named for Madame Montour, colonial interpreter. Danville was an iron manufacturing center (Montour Iron Works). Geisinger Medical Center (1915) is a major employer.
county_metadata.research_tips: Montour County Historical Society in Danville maintains genealogical files and iron industry records. Recorder of Deeds has records from 1850. For pre-1850 check Columbia County. The Montour Iron Works records document early industrial workers. Geisinger Medical Center records (from 1915) document the medical community. German and Scotch-Irish settlers documented in Lutheran, Reformed, and Presbyterian records. Thomas Beaver Free Library has local history. The small size of the county makes focused genealogical research more manageable. Cross-reference with Columbia and Northumberland counties.
county_metadata.heritage_book: History of Columbia and Montour Counties (1887)

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1752 from Bucks County. Lehigh Valley region. Easton at the confluence of the Delaware and Lehigh rivers. Bethlehem founded by Moravians (1741). Major Moravian heritage. Bethlehem Steel Company (1857-2003) was America's second-largest steel producer. Cement industry. Lafayette College in Easton.
county_metadata.research_tips: Northampton County Historical and Genealogical Society in Easton has extensive collections. The Moravian Archives in Bethlehem are world-class, holding meticulous church records from 1741 including daily diaries, membership records, and correspondence documenting every member of the Moravian community. Recorder of Deeds in Easton has records from 1752. For pre-1752 check Bucks County. Bethlehem Steel records document thousands of immigrant workers (NMIH archives). The cement industry employed many Eastern European immigrants. Lafayette College archives have local manuscripts. German Reformed and Lutheran records complement Moravian records. The Easton area has Dutch and English heritage along the Delaware River. Walking Purchase (1737) documentation affects land title research.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1752; online index. Colonial-era records

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1772 from Bedford, Cumberland, Berks, Lancaster, and Northampton counties. Major parent county for central PA. Sunbury at confluence of North and South branches of Susquehanna. Joseph Priestley (discovered oxygen) lived in Northumberland. Coal mining in eastern portion. Shamokin coal region.
county_metadata.research_tips: Northumberland County Historical Society in Sunbury maintains genealogical files and local records. Recorder of Deeds has records from 1772. As a major parent county, Northumberland records are essential for researchers in Columbia, Montour, Union, Snyder, and parts of many other counties. The anthracite coal region around Shamokin and Mount Carmel generated immigrant records. Joseph Priestley House in Northumberland has manuscript collections. Lutheran, Reformed, Presbyterian records serve valley families. The Susquehanna River communities have strong pre-Revolutionary heritage. Tax records from 1772 are invaluable for pre-census research. Cross-reference extensively with child counties for complete family documentation.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1772; colonial-era records for central PA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1820 from Cumberland County. Named for Commodore Oliver Hazard Perry. Rural mountainous county west of Harrisburg. Agricultural with some timber. Tuscarora State Forest. Conservative rural character.
county_metadata.research_tips: Perry County Historical Society in New Bloomfield maintains genealogical files. Recorder of Deeds has records from 1820. For pre-1820 check Cumberland County. The Scotch-Irish and German heritage is strong. Presbyterian and Lutheran church records are primary sources. The rural character means many families documented through deed, tax, and census records rather than church records. Cross-reference with Cumberland, Juniata, and Dauphin counties. Newport and Duncannon have distinct community records along the Juniata and Susquehanna rivers. The canal era brought some diversity to the river towns.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from county formation.
Wills, administrations from 1820.
